import React from 'react'; import { Scroll, Shield, Book, Gavel } from 'lucide-react'; const Constitution: React.FC = () => { return (

The Constitution

of the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society

{/* Article I */}

Article I: Name, Identity, and Official Branding

The official name of this organization shall be the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society, hereafter referred to as BMMSS. The organization is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University (OSU). Its identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically the Department of Mathematics and Department of Physics) and the College of Engineering.

The society shall maintain a distinct visual identity that reflects its commitment to both the university’s heritage and the precision of the mathematical sciences. The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center, signifying our pride as Buckeyes, surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits. This branding, alongside the acronym BMMSS, remains the intellectual property of the organization under the guidelines provided by the OSU Student Organization Services. All official communications, including digital publications, research papers, and competition entries, must utilize standardized BMMSS headers to ensure a professional and unified representation of the university. The society shall utilize a "Technical Scarlet" and "Digital Gray" color palette to signify the intersection of Ohio State tradition and modern technological innovation.

{/* Article II */}

Article II: Purpose, Mission, and Core Objectives

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

  1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry: BMMSS exists to provide a laboratory for students to apply abstract mathematical theorems to complex, open-ended, real-world problems. We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling. By engaging with these challenges, members develop the critical thinking skills necessary to handle the ambiguity of real-world data and physical systems.
  2. Technical Mastery and Software Literacy: We recognize that modern modeling is inseparable from computation. Therefore, a primary objective of BMMSS is to provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools such as MATLAB, Mathematica, and R, alongside programming languages including Python (specifically the NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib stacks) and C++. Furthermore, the society mandates the use of LaTeX for all formal publications to ensure that our members are prepared for the standards of professional academia.
  3. Global Competitive Presence: BMMSS serves as the official training ground for Ohio State’s participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM) and other international research symposiums. We aim to elevate the university’s global ranking in these competitions by fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.
  4. Quantitative Leadership: We champion a culture of data-driven decision-making across the university. BMMSS graduates should be recognized as leaders who can translate complex physical phenomena into actionable mathematical insights, preparing them for elite roles in national laboratories and advanced engineering firms.
{/* Article III */}

Article III: Membership, Standing, and University Compliance

Section 1: Eligibility and Inclusion

Membership in BMMSS is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at The Ohio State University, regardless of their major, department, or prior experience level. While the society is inherently technical, we value diversity of thought and encourage students from the humanities, social sciences, and arts to join if they possess an interest in the quantitative modeling of complex systems. There are no initial GPA requirements for general membership, though certain competition teams may implement merit-based selection criteria for elite divisions.

Section 2: Active Standing and Participation

To ensure the society remains a vibrant community, "Active Member" status is defined by rigorous participation requirements. An Active Member must attend a minimum of 50% of the General Assemblies and 50% of the Technical Workshops held during a single academic semester. Furthermore, Active Members must contribute to at least one society-sanctioned research project, simulation group, or competition team. Only Active Members in good standing with the university are permitted to vote in the General Assembly, hold executive office, or receive society funding for competition fees and travel subsidies.

Section 3: Mandatory University Clauses

BMMSS strictly adheres to the University Non-Discrimination and Sexual Misconduct policies. We are committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive, and professional environment free of harassment and discrimination. As a STEM-focused organization, we proactively work to ensure that all students, regardless of age, ancestry, color, disability, gender identity or expression, genetic information, HIV/AIDS status, military status, national origin, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, or protected veteran status, have equitable access to our computational resources, leadership roles, and educational workshops. Any member found in violation of the Ohio State Student Code of Conduct will be subject to immediate review and potential expulsion from the society.

{/* Article IV */}

Article IV: The Leadership Hierarchy (Executive Board & Advisor)

The leadership of BMMSS consists of an Executive Board (E-Board) of eight student officers and one Faculty Advisor, each tasked with maintaining the society’s operational integrity.

  • 1. Faculty Advisor (1): Must be a full-time OSU faculty/staff member. They serve as the "Permanent Secretary," providing institutional memory. They review technical accuracy, assist in navigating university bureaucracy (securing Math Tower labs), and act as the final signatory for official funding and registration documents. They possess "Consultative Veto Power" over actions risking the society’s legal or academic standing.
  • 2. President (1): Chief Executive Officer. Responsible for long-term strategic vision, presiding over E-Board and GA sessions, and acting as the primary representative to the Mathematics Department. The President holds tie-breaking authority in E-Board votes and oversees officer performance.
  • 3. Vice President - Internal (1): Chief Operations Officer. Manages the "Internal Ecosystem," including member retention, project team formations, and intra-club conflict resolution. They oversee the "Member-to-Project" matching system.
  • 4. Vice President - External (1): Chief Relations Officer. Manages partnerships with other departments (Physics, CSE, Engineering), industrial sponsors, and guest speakers. They coordinate site visits to research facilities and manage the alumni network.
  • 5. Secretary (1): Chief Information Officer. Maintains the official roster, records detailed meeting minutes, manages room reservations via the University Registrar, and oversees the "Weekly Simulation Dispatch" newsletter.
  • 6. Treasurer (1): Chief Financial Officer. Manages the bank account, USG funding applications, and prepares the "Annual Financial Forecast." They must audit all expenditures and provide monthly transparency reports to the E-Board.
  • 7. Tech Admin - Programmer (1): Lead Architect. Curates the technical curriculum, leads workshops on modeling algorithms (Python/MATLAB), and manages the society’s GitHub repository and documentation standards.
  • 8. Tech Admin - Webmaster (1): Chief Digital Officer. Responsible for the architecture and security of the BMMSS website, simulation visualization hosting, and digital communication tools (Discord/Slack).
  • 9. Minister of Propaganda (1): Chief Communications Officer. Directs the visual identity and narrative of BMMSS. Responsible for high-fidelity graphic design, social media growth, and large-scale recruitment campaigns (e.g., Involvement Fair).
{/* Article V */}

Article V: The General Assembly (The Bi-Annual Session)

The General Assembly (GA) is the supreme governing body of BMMSS, consisting of all Active Members.

  1. Frequency and Timing: The GA shall convene twice per academic year: once in September (Autumn Session) to ratify the annual roadmap and project groups, and once in February (Spring Session) to review competition performance and budget status.
  2. Mandatory Reporting: Every member of the Executive Board must deliver a formal work report to the GA. These reports must be quantifiable (e.g., number of workshops held, funds raised, code repositories updated). This ensures absolute transparency between leadership and the general body.
  3. Deliberation and Oversight: The GA shall debate the performance of each officer. Members reserve the constitutional right to question officers on their adherence to the society’s mission. Following the reports, a mandatory Q&A session allows Active Members to challenge E-Board decisions. The GA concludes with a vote to accept or reject the reports; a rejection triggers a mandatory review of that officer’s role by the Faculty Advisor.
{/* Article VI */}

Article VI: Accountability and Impeachment

  1. Review of Duty: Any Active Member may introduce a motion of "No Confidence" if an officer is found to be in dereliction of duty, mismanaging funds, or failing to meet the reporting standards of the General Assembly.
  2. Impeachment Process: Once a motion is seconded by five Active Members, a judicial hearing is scheduled within seven days, presided over by the Advisor. The officer in question is granted the "Right of Defense" to present evidence and respond to grievances.
  3. Final Vote: An officer may be removed from their position if a motion for impeachment is supported by a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the General Assembly via secret ballot. Grounds for removal include, but are not limited to: financial negligence, failure to attend three consecutive E-Board meetings without notice, or violation of the University Code of Student Conduct. This process ensures that leadership positions are treated as a high-stakes responsibility.
{/* Article VII */}

Article VII: Mid-Term Appointments and Vacancies

  1. Vacancies: Should a position become vacant due to resignation or impeachment, the President must announce the vacancy to the entire membership within 48 hours. If the Presidency is vacated, the VP-Internal shall immediately assume the role of "Acting President."
  2. By-Elections: A special session of the General Assembly shall be called within 14 days to fill the vacancy. All candidates must undergo a "Technical Competency Review" by the remaining E-Board to ensure they possess the skills required for the specific role (especially for Tech Admin and Treasurer roles).
  3. Interim Governance: The President may appoint an "Acting Officer" for a maximum of 14 days to maintain essential services (e.g., website hosting or treasury access) until the GA vote is concluded. This ensures that the society's infrastructure remains operational during leadership transitions.
{/* Article VIII */}

Article VIII: Project Standards and Technical Ethics

  1. Code of Ethics: All models and simulations produced under the BMMSS name must adhere to strict academic integrity standards. Plagiarism in code or mathematical theory results in immediate expulsion and a report to the OSU Committee on Academic Misconduct (COAM).
  2. Open Source and Documentation: To benefit the OSU community, the Tech Admin - Programmer shall ensure project code is documented and stored in the BMMSS Open Repository. This initiative democratizes knowledge and ensures the society’s work survives long-term.
  3. Competition Preparation: The society shall dedicate the period from November to February specifically to "Competitive Modeling Mode." This includes intensive 48-hour mock sessions and rigorous peer-review of paper drafts to ensure BMMSS maintains its reputation as a global competitor in the MCM/ICM.
{/* Article IX */}

Article IX: Financial Governance and Transparency

  1. Transparency: The Treasurer must maintain a "Live Ledger" accessible to the E-Board. A condensed version, the "Quarterly Financial Snapshot," must be presented to the GA bi-annually. This ensures members know exactly how their dues and USG grants are utilized.
  2. Expenditure Authority: We implement a tiered approval system. Routine spends under $50 require Treasurer approval. Spends between $50 and $200 require joint President-Treasurer approval. Any capital expenditure exceeding $200 (e.g., server hardware or high-end licenses) requires a majority vote of the full E-Board and a "Value Assessment."
  3. Audit: At the end of each Spring semester, the Faculty Advisor shall conduct a formal audit of the society’s accounts. The audit report must be filed with the OSU Student Organization Finance Office (SOFO) to ensure compliance with university regulations and to facilitate a smooth handover to the next Treasurer.
{/* Article X */}

Article X: Amendments and Dissolution

  1. Amendments: This constitution is a living document. Amendments may be proposed by any Active Member and require a written rationale. Adoption requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the General Assembly during the September or February sessions.
  2. Dissolution Protocol: Dissolution requires a three-fourths (3/4) vote of the GA and must follow all OSU exit procedures. In the event of dissolution, all physical assets purchased with university funds shall be returned to the OSU Department of Mathematics. Digital repositories shall be placed into a public "Legacy Archive" to ensure the scientific community can continue to benefit from BMMSS research. Remaining private funds shall be donated to a scholarship fund within the Department of Mathematics.
); }; export default Constitution;

{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

); case 'leadership': return (

Leadership Hierarchy

As defined in Article IV of the Constitution, the Executive Board consists of eight student officers and one Faculty Advisor. We are actively recruiting for the vacant positions below for our Fall 2026 launch.

); case 'events': return (

Events

Our full programming will begin in Fall 2026. BMMSS operates on a bi-annual session basis, with a General Assembly in September (Autumn Session) and February (Spring Session).

); case 'attendance': return ; case 'ai-lab': return (

AI Modeling Lab

Get a head start on your preparation. Leverage the power of Google's Gemini models to generate practice problems for future competitions.

); case 'contact': return (

import React from 'react'; import { Scroll, Shield, Book, Gavel } from 'lucide-react'; const Constitution: React.FC = () => { return (

The Constitution

of the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society

{/* Article I */}

Article I: Name, Identity, and Official Branding

The official name of this organization shall be the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society, hereafter referred to as BMMSS. The organization is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University (OSU). Its identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically the Department of Mathematics and Department of Physics) and the College of Engineering.

The society shall maintain a distinct visual identity that reflects its commitment to both the university’s heritage and the precision of the mathematical sciences. The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center, signifying our pride as Buckeyes, surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits. This branding, alongside the acronym BMMSS, remains the intellectual property of the organization under the guidelines provided by the OSU Student Organization Services. All official communications, including digital publications, research papers, and competition entries, must utilize standardized BMMSS headers to ensure a professional and unified representation of the university. The society shall utilize a "Technical Scarlet" and "Digital Gray" color palette to signify the intersection of Ohio State tradition and modern technological innovation.

{/* Article II */}

Article II: Purpose, Mission, and Core Objectives

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

  1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry: BMMSS exists to provide a laboratory for students to apply abstract mathematical theorems to complex, open-ended, real-world problems. We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling. By engaging with these challenges, members develop the critical thinking skills necessary to handle the ambiguity of real-world data and physical systems.
  2. Technical Mastery and Software Literacy: We recognize that modern modeling is inseparable from computation. Therefore, a primary objective of BMMSS is to provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools such as MATLAB, Mathematica, and R, alongside programming languages including Python (specifically the NumPy, SciPy, and Matplotlib stacks) and C++. Furthermore, the society mandates the use of LaTeX for all formal publications to ensure that our members are prepared for the standards of professional academia.
  3. Global Competitive Presence: BMMSS serves as the official training ground for Ohio State’s participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM) and other international research symposiums. We aim to elevate the university’s global ranking in these competitions by fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.
  4. Quantitative Leadership: We champion a culture of data-driven decision-making across the university. BMMSS graduates should be recognized as leaders who can translate complex physical phenomena into actionable mathematical insights, preparing them for elite roles in national laboratories and advanced engineering firms.
{/* Article III */}

Article III: Membership, Standing, and University Compliance

Section 1: Eligibility and Inclusion

Membership in BMMSS is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at The Ohio State University, regardless of their major, department, or prior experience level. While the society is inherently technical, we value diversity of thought and encourage students from the humanities, social sciences, and arts to join if they possess an interest in the quantitative modeling of complex systems. There are no initial GPA requirements for general membership, though certain competition teams may implement merit-based selection criteria for elite divisions.

Section 2: Active Standing and Participation

To ensure the society remains a vibrant community, "Active Member" status is defined by rigorous participation requirements. An Active Member must attend a minimum of 50% of the General Assemblies and 50% of the Technical Workshops held during a single academic semester. Furthermore, Active Members must contribute to at least one society-sanctioned research project, simulation group, or competition team. Only Active Members in good standing with the university are permitted to vote in the General Assembly, hold executive office, or receive society funding for competition fees and travel subsidies.

Section 3: Mandatory University Clauses

BMMSS strictly adheres to the University Non-Discrimination and Sexual Misconduct policies. We are committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive, and professional environment free of harassment and discrimination. As a STEM-focused organization, we proactively work to ensure that all students, regardless of age, ancestry, color, disability, gender identity or expression, genetic information, HIV/AIDS status, military status, national origin, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, or protected veteran status, have equitable access to our computational resources, leadership roles, and educational workshops. Any member found in violation of the Ohio State Student Code of Conduct will be subject to immediate review and potential expulsion from the society.

{/* Article IV */}

Article IV: The Leadership Hierarchy (Executive Board & Advisor)

The leadership of BMMSS consists of an Executive Board (E-Board) of nine student officers and one Faculty Advisor, each tasked with maintaining the society’s operational integrity.

  • 1. Faculty Advisor (1): Must be a full-time OSU faculty/staff member. They serve as the "Permanent Secretary," providing institutional memory. They review technical accuracy, assist in navigating university bureaucracy (securing Math Tower labs), and act as the final signatory for official funding and registration documents. They possess "Consultative Veto Power" over actions risking the society’s legal or academic standing.
  • 2. President (1): Chief Executive Officer. Responsible for long-term strategic vision, presiding over E-Board and GA sessions, and acting as the primary representative to the Mathematics Department. The President holds tie-breaking authority in E-Board votes and oversees officer performance.
  • 3. Vice President - Internal (1): Chief Operations Officer. Manages the "Internal Ecosystem," including member retention, project team formations, and intra-club conflict resolution. They oversee the "Member-to-Project" matching system.
  • 4. Vice President - External (1): Chief Relations Officer. Manages partnerships with other departments (Physics, CSE, Engineering), industrial sponsors, and guest speakers. They coordinate site visits to research facilities and manage the alumni network.
  • 5. Secretary (1): Chief Information Officer. Maintains the official roster, records detailed meeting minutes, manages room reservations via the University Registrar, and oversees the "Weekly Simulation Dispatch" newsletter.
  • 6. Treasurer (1): Chief Financial Officer. Manages the bank account, USG funding applications, and prepares the "Annual Financial Forecast." They must audit all expenditures and provide monthly transparency reports to the E-Board.
  • 7. Tech Admin - Programmer (1): Lead Architect. Curates the technical curriculum, leads workshops on modeling algorithms (Python/MATLAB), and manages the society’s GitHub repository and documentation standards.
  • 8. Tech Admin - Webmaster (1): Chief Digital Officer. Responsible for the architecture and security of the BMMSS website, simulation visualization hosting, and digital communication tools (Discord/Slack).
  • 9. Human Resources Director (1): Chief People Officer. Responsible for the internal well-being of the society, mediating conflicts, fostering diversity, and managing recruitment pipelines to ensure interdisciplinary integration (e.g., Sociology and Humanities).
  • 10. Minister of Propaganda (1): Chief Communications Officer. Directs the visual identity and narrative of BMMSS. Responsible for high-fidelity graphic design, social media growth, and large-scale recruitment campaigns (e.g., Involvement Fair).
{/* Article V */}

Article V: The General Assembly (The Bi-Annual Session)

The General Assembly (GA) is the supreme governing body of BMMSS, consisting of all Active Members.

  1. Frequency and Timing: The GA shall convene twice per academic year: once in September (Autumn Session) to ratify the annual roadmap and project groups, and once in February (Spring Session) to review competition performance and budget status.
  2. Mandatory Reporting: Every member of the Executive Board must deliver a formal work report to the GA. These reports must be quantifiable (e.g., number of workshops held, funds raised, code repositories updated). This ensures absolute transparency between leadership and the general body.
  3. Deliberation and Oversight: The GA shall debate the performance of each officer. Members reserve the constitutional right to question officers on their adherence to the society’s mission. Following the reports, a mandatory Q&A session allows Active Members to challenge E-Board decisions. The GA concludes with a vote to accept or reject the reports; a rejection triggers a mandatory review of that officer’s role by the Faculty Advisor.
{/* Article VI */}

Article VI: Accountability and Impeachment

  1. Review of Duty: Any Active Member may introduce a motion of "No Confidence" if an officer is found to be in dereliction of duty, mismanaging funds, or failing to meet the reporting standards of the General Assembly.
  2. Impeachment Process: Once a motion is seconded by five Active Members, a judicial hearing is scheduled within seven days, presided over by the Advisor. The officer in question is granted the "Right of Defense" to present evidence and respond to grievances.
  3. Final Vote: An officer may be removed from their position if a motion for impeachment is supported by a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the General Assembly via secret ballot. Grounds for removal include, but are not limited to: financial negligence, failure to attend three consecutive E-Board meetings without notice, or violation of the University Code of Student Conduct. This process ensures that leadership positions are treated as a high-stakes responsibility.
{/* Article VII */}

Article VII: Mid-Term Appointments and Vacancies

  1. Vacancies: Should a position become vacant due to resignation or impeachment, the President must announce the vacancy to the entire membership within 48 hours. If the Presidency is vacated, the VP-Internal shall immediately assume the role of "Acting President."
  2. By-Elections: A special session of the General Assembly shall be called within 14 days to fill the vacancy. All candidates must undergo a "Technical Competency Review" by the remaining E-Board to ensure they possess the skills required for the specific role (especially for Tech Admin and Treasurer roles).
  3. Interim Governance: The President may appoint an "Acting Officer" for a maximum of 14 days to maintain essential services (e.g., website hosting or treasury access) until the GA vote is concluded. This ensures that the society's infrastructure remains operational during leadership transitions.
{/* Article VIII */}

Article VIII: Project Standards and Technical Ethics

  1. Code of Ethics: All models and simulations produced under the BMMSS name must adhere to strict academic integrity standards. Plagiarism in code or mathematical theory results in immediate expulsion and a report to the OSU Committee on Academic Misconduct (COAM).
  2. Open Source and Documentation: To benefit the OSU community, the Tech Admin - Programmer shall ensure project code is documented and stored in the BMMSS Open Repository. This initiative democratizes knowledge and ensures the society’s work survives long-term.
  3. Competition Preparation: The society shall dedicate the period from November to February specifically to "Competitive Modeling Mode." This includes intensive 48-hour mock sessions and rigorous peer-review of paper drafts to ensure BMMSS maintains its reputation as a global competitor in the MCM/ICM.
{/* Article IX */}

Article IX: Financial Governance and Transparency

  1. Transparency: The Treasurer must maintain a "Live Ledger" accessible to the E-Board. A condensed version, the "Quarterly Financial Snapshot," must be presented to the GA bi-annually. This ensures members know exactly how their dues and USG grants are utilized.
  2. Expenditure Authority: We implement a tiered approval system. Routine spends under $50 require Treasurer approval. Spends between $50 and $200 require joint President-Treasurer approval. Any capital expenditure exceeding $200 (e.g., server hardware or high-end licenses) requires a majority vote of the full E-Board and a "Value Assessment."
  3. Audit: At the end of each Spring semester, the Faculty Advisor shall conduct a formal audit of the society’s accounts. The audit report must be filed with the OSU Student Organization Finance Office (SOFO) to ensure compliance with university regulations and to facilitate a smooth handover to the next Treasurer.
{/* Article X */}

Article X: Amendments and Dissolution

  1. Amendments: This constitution is a living document. Amendments may be proposed by any Active Member and require a written rationale. Adoption requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the General Assembly during the September or February sessions.
  2. Dissolution Protocol: Dissolution requires a three-fourths (3/4) vote of the GA and must follow all OSU exit procedures. In the event of dissolution, all physical assets purchased with university funds shall be returned to the OSU Department of Mathematics. Digital repositories shall be placed into a public "Legacy Archive" to ensure the scientific community can continue to benefit from BMMSS research. Remaining private funds shall be donated to a scholarship fund within the Department of Mathematics.
); }; export default Constitution;

{/* Hero Section */}

Modeling the Future{' '} Simulating Reality

Welcome to the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS). We are an interdisciplinary nexus bridging the gap between advanced mathematical theory and high-performance computational simulation.

🚀 Launching Fall 2026
{/* Dynamic 3D Heatmap Visualization */}
{/* Features Section */}

Core Objectives

Analyze, Compute, Compete

Our constitution defines four strategic pillars that guide our mission to transcend traditional classroom boundaries.

Rigorous Inquiry

Moving beyond "toy problems" into stochastic processes and non-linear dynamics.

Technical Mastery

Systematic training in MATLAB, Python (NumPy/SciPy), and LaTeX.

Global Presence

Official training ground for the COMAP MCM/ICM and international symposiums.

Quant Leadership

Translating complex physical phenomena into actionable mathematical insights.

); case 'about': return (
{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

); case 'leadership': return (

Leadership Hierarchy

As defined in Article IV of the Constitution, the Executive Board consists of eight student officers and one Faculty Advisor. We are actively recruiting for the vacant positions below for our Fall 2026 launch.

); case 'events': return (

Events

Our full programming will begin in Fall 2026. BMMSS operates on a bi-annual session basis, with a General Assembly in September (Autumn Session) and February (Spring Session).

); case 'attendance': return ; case 'ai-lab': return (

AI Modeling Lab

Get a head start on your preparation. Leverage the power of Google's Gemini models to generate practice problems for future competitions.

); case 'contact': return (

We are currently building our core team for the Fall 2026 launch. If you are interested in becoming a founding officer or member, please reach out!

President: Sicheng Qian qian.917@osu.edu

{/* Hero Section */}

Modeling the Future{' '} Simulating Reality

Welcome to the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS). We are an interdisciplinary nexus bridging the gap between advanced mathematical theory and high-performance computational simulation.

🚀 Launching Fall 2026
{/* Dynamic 3D Heatmap Visualization */}
{/* Features Section */}

Core Objectives

Analyze, Compute, Compete

Our constitution defines four strategic pillars that guide our mission to transcend traditional classroom boundaries.

Rigorous Inquiry

Moving beyond "toy problems" into stochastic processes and non-linear dynamics.

Technical Mastery

Systematic training in MATLAB, Python (NumPy/SciPy), and LaTeX.

Global Presence

Official training ground for the COMAP MCM/ICM and international symposiums.

Quant Leadership

Translating complex physical phenomena into actionable mathematical insights.

); case 'about': return (
{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

); case 'leadership': return (

Leadership Hierarchy

As defined in Article IV of the Constitution, the Executive Board consists of eight student officers and one Faculty Advisor. We are actively recruiting for the vacant positions below for our Fall 2026 launch.

); case 'events': return (

Events

Our full programming will begin in Fall 2026. BMMSS operates on a bi-annual session basis, with a General Assembly in September (Autumn Session) and February (Spring Session).

); case 'attendance': return ; case 'ai-lab': return (

AI Modeling Lab

); case 'contact': return (

We are currently building our core team for the Fall 2026 launch. If you are interested in becoming a founding officer or member, please reach out!

{/* Hero Section */}

Modeling the Future{' '} Simulating Reality

Welcome to the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS). We are an interdisciplinary nexus bridging the gap between advanced mathematical theory and high-performance computational simulation.

🚀 Launching Fall 2026

Analyze, Compute, Compete

); case 'about': return (
{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

); case 'leadership': return (

Leadership Hierarchy

As defined in Article IV of the Constitution, the Executive Board consists of eight student officers and one Faculty Advisor. We are actively recruiting for the vacant positions below for our Fall 2026 launch.

); case 'events': return (

Events

Our full programming will begin in Fall 2026. BMMSS operates on a bi-annual session basis, with a General Assembly in September (Autumn Session) and February (Spring Session).

); case 'attendance': return ; case 'ai-lab': return (

AI Modeling Lab

Get a head start on your preparation. Leverage the power of Google's Gemini models to generate practice problems for future competitions.

); case 'contact': return (

Get Involved

We are currently building our core team for the Fall 2026 launch. If you are interested in becoming a founding officer or member, please reach out!

President: Sicheng Qian qian.917@osu.edu
github.com/bmmss-osu

Application Form

{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

payload.text:
**Revising BMMSS Website**

I've begun the website update for the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society. I'm focusing on incorporating the details extracted from the updated constitution document into the website's content and structure. My aim is to make the information clear and easy to find.


**Refining Constitution Integration**

I'm now integrating the full text of the constitution into the `Constitution.tsx` component. I've updated all instances of "Article IV" in the document to "Article VI" to reflect the new constitution. The `App.tsx` file has been updated to reflect the change as well. My current work involves ensuring the layout and structure are consistent with the existing site aesthetics, and creating distinct articles for Name, Purpose, University Regulations, Membership, Advisor, Organization Leadership, Dissolution, and Amendments.


**Reconciling Leadership Roles**

I'm now reconciling the leadership roles with the new constitution. The current `LeadershipSection.tsx` has 8 roles, but the constitution specifies 7. I've cross-referenced the PDF and identified the discrepancy: the current website lists the "Programmer" and "Webmaster" as separate roles, while the constitution groups them as "Tech Leader" but specifically lists "Tech Leader: Modeling Leader" and "Tech Leader: Webmaster". I'll be updating `LeadershipSection.tsx` to reflect this structure. I am removing "Programmer".


**Adjusting Leadership Display**

I'm now focusing on the leadership section. I'm noting the discrepancy between the constitution's specified 7 roles and the website's displayed 8. Considering the user's past input, I'll update `Constitution.tsx` to exactly reflect the PDF, which specifies a single VP. However, for the displayed leadership on the website, I'll retain the 8 positions with the users specified names, keeping both VPs. This approach aims to reconcile the official constitution with the user's specific preferences, ensuring accuracy and consistency.

To ensure the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society website is perfectly aligned with the newly provided legal governing document, I will update the Constitution page with the exact text and structure from your 4-page PDF. {/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react'; import { Scroll, Shield, Book, Gavel } from 'lucide-react'; const Constitution: React.FC = () => { return (

The Constitution

of the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society

{/* Article I */}

Article I. Name of Organization

The official name of this organization shall be the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society, hereafter referred to as BMMSS.

{/* Article II */}

Article II. Organization Purpose

The purpose of BMMSS is to cultivate a community of "Real-World Problem Solvers" at The Ohio State University. The society aims to bridge the gap between theoretical mathematical education and practical industrial application by:

  1. Developing computational models to solve tangible campus and community challenges.
  2. Providing a collaborative platform for students of all levels (undergraduate and graduate) to master simulation tools and mathematical rigor.
  3. Fostering interdisciplinary innovation through a mentorship-driven research environment.
{/* Article III */}

Article III. University Regulations

Section 1 — Non-Discrimination Statement:

This organization does not discriminate on the basis of age, ancestry, color, disability, gender identity or expression, genetic information, HIV/AIDS status, military status, national origin, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, or any other bases under the law, in its activities, programs, admission, and employment.

Section 2 — Sexual Misconduct Policy:

As a student organization at The Ohio State University, BMMSS expects its members to conduct themselves in a manner that maintains an environment free from sexual misconduct. All members are responsible for adhering to the University's Policy on Sexual Misconduct.

{/* Article IV */}

Article IV. Membership

Section 1 — Membership Eligibility:

Membership is open to all currently enrolled students at The Ohio State University. While the focus is on Mathematics, Engineering, and Data Science, BMMSS encourages interdisciplinary participation.

Section 2 — Member Selection:

Prospective members must complete a brief application demonstrating a commitment to the society's mission of "real-world problem-solving." Selection is based on technical curiosity, passion for applied modeling, and willingness to participate in a mentorship-driven structure.

Section 3 — Membership Timeline:

General recruitment shall occur at the beginning of the Autumn semester. Membership remains active as long as the student is enrolled at the University and meets the participation requirements defined in the Bylaws.

Section 4 — Member Removal:

Members may be removed for conduct violating University regulations or for consistent failure to contribute to assigned projects. Removal requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the Executive Board after the member has been given an opportunity to address the concerns.

{/* Article V */}

Article V. Advisor

Section 1 — Advisor Duties and Responsibilities:

The Advisor shall provide academic guidance, oversee the technical rigor of projects, and ensure the organization's activities align with the University's mission. The Advisor will also assist in navigating departmental resources and administrative approvals.

Section 2 — Advisor Term:

The Advisor shall serve an indefinite term, reviewed annually, as long as both the Advisor and the Executive Board find the partnership mutually beneficial.

Section 3 — Advisor Selection:

The Advisor must be a full-time member of the University faculty or Administrative & Professional staff. Selection is made by the President and confirmed by the Executive Board.

Section 4 — Advisor Replacement:

Should the Advisor be unable to fulfill their duties, the Executive Board will seek a replacement through the Mathematics Department or related STEM faculties.

{/* Article VI */}

Article VI. Organization Leadership

Section 1 — Officer Positions:

The Executive Board shall consist of the following seven (7) core positions, each with distinct mandates to support the society's operational and technical excellence.

  • Primary Leader (President): Serves as the visionary and legal head of BMMSS.
    • Leads strategic planning and acts as the primary liaison with the Faculty Advisor and corporate sponsors (e.g., Coke, Siemens).
    • Ensures all projects align with the "Real-World Problem Solver" mission.
  • Secondary Leader (Vice President):
    • Oversees internal operations and ensures project timelines are met.
    • Manages the recruitment and integration of new members.
    • Assumes the President's duties in their absence.
  • Treasurer:
    • Handles all fiscal matters, including university funding requests, corporate sponsorships, and budget allocations.
    • Maintains the organization's bank account and ensures financial transparency in accordance with OSU's Council on Student Affairs (CSA) guidelines.
  • Tech Leader: Modeling Leader:
    • Ensures the mathematical rigor and technical quality of all research projects.
    • Mentors junior members and coordinates the "Mentorship Model," connecting senior leads with underclassmen.
    • Oversees the technical stack (e.g., Python, MATLAB, SimPy).
  • Tech Leader: Webmaster:
    • Designs and maintains the official BMMSS digital platform and internal project database.
    • Manages the organization's GitHub repository and digital project showcases.
    • Ensures the security and accessibility of internal modeling tools.
  • Director of Public Relations (PR):
    • Manages the BMMSS brand, social media presence, and campus outreach.
    • Drafts sponsorship decks and coordinates with the President on industry engagement.
    • Leads marketing efforts for recruitment and the annual symposium.
  • Clerk:
    • Maintains all official records, including meeting minutes, membership rosters, and constitutional updates.
    • Manages the society's calendar and coordinates logistics for weekly research workshops.
    • Ensures compliance with all university administrative deadlines.

Section 2 — Officer Eligibility:

To be eligible for an Executive Board position, a student must be a currently enrolled student at The Ohio State University and maintain a minimum cumulative GPA as required by the university. Candidates for Tech Leader positions must demonstrate proficiency in relevant modeling or digital tools.

Section 3 — Officer Selection Process:

Officers shall be elected annually during the Spring semester for the following academic year. Candidates must submit a "Project Vision Statement" detailing how they will advance the society's mission. Elections are conducted via a majority vote of active members. For the inaugural 2026-2027 year, the Founding Board shall be appointed by the President in consultation with the Advisor.

Section 4 — Officer Removal:

Any officer may be removed from their position for failure to fulfill their duties or for conduct detrimental to the organization. Removal requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the Executive Board and consultation with the Advisor.

{/* Article VII */}

Article VII. Organization Dissolution

Section 1 — Dissolution Requirements:

Dissolution of BMMSS requires a unanimous vote of the Executive Board and consultation with the Advisor.

Section 2 — Dissolution Procedures, including Assets and Debts:

Upon dissolution, any remaining organizational funds shall be returned to the Department of Mathematics or the primary sponsoring department. All outstanding debts must be settled using the organization's current assets before the final closure of the account.

{/* Article VIII */}

Article VIII. Constitutional Amendments

Section 1 — Amendment Process:

Proposed amendments to this Constitution must be submitted in writing to the Executive Board. Amendments require a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the active Executive Board and approval from the Advisor to be enacted.

); }; export default Constitution;

3.  **App Interface**:

payload.text: 为什么outlook calendar没法嵌入，refuse to connect？

**Investigating Outlook Embedding**

I'm now focusing on the likely culprit: security headers. I suspect either `X-Frame-Options` or `Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors` on Microsoft's end are preventing the calendar from embedding due to its refusal to connect. This is common with numerous Microsoft services.


**Analyzing Embedding Restrictions**

I've realized the primary hurdle is Outlook's security measures; it explicitly blocks iframe embedding to prevent clickjacking. The provided URL points to the full web app, which enforces these restrictions. A shared calendar link is the correct way, but if that is unavailable, a fallback/button is necessary. I'm preparing an explanation of why this is and a potential component update to honestly describe these limitations, should we not receive a "publishable" link.


**Clarifying Embedding Solutions**

I'm now zeroing in on a solution for the embedding issue, which appears to stem from Outlook's security settings and clickjacking prevention. To overcome this, I'm updating `EventsSection.tsx` to handle the iframe failure gracefully. I'll add a card-based visual placeholder and emphasize the external link. I'll include a code note to explain the reason for the failure. I'll also add a clearer explanation and suggest using Outlook's "Publish" feature to get an embeddable HTML link that functions with an iframe.

The "Refuse to connect" error (often shown as `ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE`) occurs because Microsoft sets a security header called **`X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN`** or a **`Content-Security-Policy`** that explicitly forbids their full web application (`outlook.office.com`) from being embedded inside an `<iframe>` on other websites. This is a security measure to prevent "clickjacking" attacks.

To actually embed a functional Outlook calendar, you usually need to use a **"Published Calendar"** link.

### How to get an embeddable link:
1. Go to Outlook Web settings (Gear icon) -> **View all Outlook settings**.
2. Go to **Calendar** -> **Shared calendars**.
3. Under **Publish a calendar**, select the calendar and choose "Can view all details".
4. Click **Publish**.
5. Copy the **HTML link** provided. That link usually starts with `https://outlook.office365.com/owa/calendar/.../calendar.html` and *is* designed to be embedded.

I have updated the `EventsSection.tsx` to handle this more gracefully by providing a clearer "Click to Access" interface, as the URL you provided will almost always be blocked by Microsoft's security policies.

{/* Hero Section */}

Modeling the Future{' '} Simulating Reality

Welcome to the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS). We are an interdisciplinary nexus bridging the gap between advanced mathematical theory and high-performance computational simulation.

🚀 Launching Fall 2026
{/* Dynamic 3D Heatmap Visualization */}
{/* Features Section */}

Core Objectives

Analyze, Compute, Compete

Our constitution defines four strategic pillars that guide our mission to transcend traditional classroom boundaries.

Rigorous Inquiry

Moving beyond "toy problems" into stochastic processes and non-linear dynamics.

Technical Mastery

Systematic training in MATLAB, Python (NumPy/SciPy), and LaTeX.

Global Presence

Official training ground for the COMAP MCM/ICM and international symposiums.

Quant Leadership

Translating complex physical phenomena into actionable mathematical insights.

); case 'about': return (
{/* Article I: Identity */}

Identity & Branding

The Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society (BMMSS) is established as a premier student-led academic and professional society at The Ohio State University.

Our identity is defined by the rigorous synthesis of advanced mathematical theory, theoretical physics, and high-performance computational simulation. BMMSS operates as an interdisciplinary nexus, bridging the gap between the College of Arts and Sciences (specifically Mathematics and Physics) and the College of Engineering.

"The official seal of BMMSS shall feature Brutus Buckeye at the center... surrounded by symbolic representations of modeling—such as phase-space trajectories, complex network nodes, or stylized atomic orbits." — Article I

{/* Article II: Purpose */}

Mission & Core Pillars

The mission of BMMSS is to establish a rigorous, high-level intellectual environment that empowers students to transcend traditional classroom boundaries. Our purpose is defined by four core strategic pillars:

1. Rigorous Academic Inquiry

We aim to move beyond "toy problems" into the realm of stochastic processes, non-linear dynamics, and multi-scale modeling, developing the critical thinking necessary to handle real-world ambiguity.

2. Technical Mastery

We provide systematic, peer-led training in industry-standard tools (MATLAB, R, Mathematica) and programming stacks (Python: NumPy, SciPy; C++). We mandate the use of LaTeX for all formal publications.

3. Global Competitive Presence

BMMSS serves as the official training ground for OSU's participation in the COMAP Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM), fostering a year-round culture of simulation and white-paper drafting.

4. Quantitative Leadership

We champion data-driven decision-making. Our graduates are leaders who translate complex physical phenomena into actionable insights, prepared for roles in national laboratories and elite firms.

{/* Article III: Membership */}

Membership

Membership is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at OSU, regardless of major. While inherently technical, we value diversity of thought from the humanities and arts.

Active Standing

"Active Member" status requires attending 50% of General Assemblies and Workshops, plus contribution to at least one society-sanctioned research project or competition team.

); case 'leadership': return (

Leadership Hierarchy

As defined in Article VI of the Constitution, the Executive Board consists of seven (7) core positions and one Faculty Advisor. We are actively recruiting for the vacant positions below for our Fall 2026 launch.

); case 'events': return (

Events

Our full programming will begin in Fall 2026. BMMSS operates on a bi-annual session basis, with a General Assembly in September (Autumn Session) and February (Spring Session).

); case 'press': return ; case 'ai-lab': return (

AI Modeling Lab

Get a head start on your preparation. Leverage the power of Google's Gemini models to generate practice problems for future competitions.

); case 'contact': return (

Get Involved

We are currently building our core team for the Fall 2026 launch. If you are interested in becoming a founding officer or member, please reach out!

President: Sicheng Qian qian.917@osu.edu
github.com/bmmss-osu

import React from 'react'; import { Scroll, Shield, Book, Gavel, FileText, Scale } from 'lucide-react'; const Constitution: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header */}

Official Document

The Constitution

of the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society

Effective 2026-2027 Academic Year
{/* Article I */}

Article I. Name of Organization

The official name of this organization shall be the Buckeyes Mathematical Modeling and Simulation Society, hereafter referred to as BMMSS.

{/* Article II */}

Article II. Organization Purpose

The purpose of BMMSS is to cultivate a community of "Real-World Problem Solvers" at The Ohio State University. The society aims to bridge the gap between theoretical mathematical education and practical industrial application by:

  1. 1 Developing computational models to solve tangible campus and community challenges.
  2. 2 Providing a collaborative platform for students of all levels (undergraduate and graduate) to master simulation tools and mathematical rigor.
  3. 3 Fostering interdisciplinary innovation through a mentorship-driven research environment.
{/* Article III */}

Article III. University Regulations

Section 1 — Non-Discrimination Statement:

This organization does not discriminate on the basis of age, ancestry, color, disability, gender identity or expression, genetic information, HIV/AIDS status, military status, national origin, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, or any other bases under the law, in its activities, programs, admission, and employment.

Section 2 — Sexual Misconduct Policy:

As a student organization at The Ohio State University, BMMSS expects its members to conduct themselves in a manner that maintains an environment free from sexual misconduct. All members are responsible for adhering to the University’s Policy on Sexual Misconduct.

{/* Article IV */}

Article IV. Membership

Section 1 — Membership Eligibility:

Membership is open to all currently enrolled students at The Ohio State University. While the focus is on Mathematics, Engineering, and Data Science, BMMSS encourages interdisciplinary participation.

Section 2 — Member Selection:

While general membership is open, participation in specific Project Teams or Executive Leadership roles may require a separate application or selection process to ensure technical alignment and project continuity.

Section 3 — Membership Timeline:

General membership remains active as long as the student is enrolled at the University and adheres to the code of conduct.

Section 4 — Member Removal:

Members may be removed for conduct violating University regulations or for consistent failure to contribute to assigned projects. Removal requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the Executive Board after the member has been given an opportunity to address the concerns.

{/* Article V */}

Article V. Advisor

Section 1 — Advisor Duties and Responsibilities:

The Advisor shall provide general guidance, academic support, and mentorship to the organization. The Advisor serves as a resource for the technical direction of the society and ensures that activities align with the University’s educational mission. The Advisor’s role is strictly advisory and does not include voting rights in organizational decisions.

Section 2 — Advisor Term:

The Advisor shall serve an indefinite term, reviewed annually, as long as both the Advisor and the Executive Board find the partnership mutually beneficial.

Section 3 — Advisor Selection:

The Advisor must be a full-time member of the University faculty or Administrative & Professional staff. Selection is made by the President and confirmed by the Executive Board.

Section 4 — Advisor Replacement:

Should the Advisor be unable to fulfill their duties, the Executive Board will seek a replacement through the Mathematics Department or related STEM faculties.

{/* Article VI */}

Article VI. Organization Leadership

Section 1 — Officer Positions:

The Executive Board shall consist of the following seven (7) core positions, each with distinct mandates to support the society’s operational and technical excellence.

Primary Leader (President): *

  • Serves as the visionary and legal head of BMMSS.
  • Leads strategic planning and acts as the primary liaison with the Faculty Advisor and corporate sponsors (e.g., Coke, Siemens).
  • Ensures all projects align with the "Real-World Problem Solver" mission.

Secondary Leader (Vice President):

  • Oversees internal operations and ensures project timelines are met.
  • Manages the recruitment and integration of new members.
  • Assumes the President's duties in their absence.

Treasurer:

  • Handles all fiscal matters, including university funding requests, corporate sponsorships, and budget allocations.
  • Maintains the organization’s bank account and ensures financial transparency in accordance with OSU’s Council on Student Affairs (CSA) guidelines.

Tech Leader: Modeling Leader:

  • Ensures the mathematical rigor and technical quality of all research projects.
  • Mentors junior members and coordinates the "Mentorship Model," connecting senior leads with underclassmen.
  • Oversees the technical stack (e.g., Python, MATLAB, SimPy).

Tech Leader: Webmaster:

  • Designs and maintains the official BMMSS digital platform and internal project database.
  • Manages the organization’s GitHub repository and digital project showcases.
  • Ensures the security and accessibility of internal modeling tools.

Director of Public Relations (PR):

  • Manages the BMMSS brand, social media presence, and campus outreach.
  • Drafts sponsorship decks and coordinates with the President on industry engagement.
  • Leads marketing efforts for recruitment and the annual symposium.

Clerk:

  • Maintains all official records, including meeting minutes, membership rosters, and constitutional updates.
  • Manages the society’s calendar and coordinates logistics for weekly research workshops.
  • Ensures compliance with all university administrative deadlines.

Section 2 — Officer Eligibility:

To be eligible for an Executive Board position, a student must be a currently enrolled student at The Ohio State University and maintain a minimum cumulative GPA as required by the university. Candidates for Tech Leader positions must demonstrate proficiency in relevant modeling or digital tools.

Section 3 — Officer Selection Process:

Officers shall be elected annually during the Spring semester for the following academic year. Candidates must submit a "Project Vision Statement" detailing how they will advance the society's mission. Elections are conducted via a majority vote of active members. For the inaugural 2026-2027 year, the Founding Board shall be appointed by the President in consultation with the Advisor.

Section 4 — Officer Removal:

Any officer may be removed from their position for failure to fulfill their duties or for conduct detrimental to the organization. Removal requires a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the Executive Board and consultation with the Advisor.

{/* Article VII */}

Article VII. Organization Dissolution

Section 1 — Dissolution Requirements:

Dissolution of BMMSS requires a unanimous vote of the Executive Board and consultation with the Advisor.

Section 2 — Dissolution Procedures, including Assets and Debts:

Upon dissolution, any remaining organizational funds shall be returned to the Department of Mathematics or the primary sponsoring department. All outstanding debts must be settled using the organization's current assets before the final closure of the account.

{/* Article VIII */}

Article VIII. Constitutional Amendments

Section 1 — Amendment Process:

Proposed amendments must be submitted in writing to the Executive Board. Enacting an amendment requires:

  • ●
    Executive Approval: A two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of the active Executive Board.
  • ●
    Membership Ratification: A majority vote of the general membership present at a scheduled meeting, provided that a quorum of at least 25% of the total active membership is represented.
  • ●
    Advisor Concurrence: Review and non-objection by the Advisor to ensure alignment with University and Departmental policies.
